findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "subscribe_newsletter" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Subscribe an email address to the Eveoy newsletter (case studies + lookbooks). Use this when the user EXPLICITLY asks to subscribe or sign up for updates. Trigg…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

labels.name: check_order_status
labels.description:
Look up the status of an Eveoy order by its Stripe Checkout session id.

Use this when the user wants to:
- Check whether an order was paid
- See the order total and size for a checkout session

Trigger phrases include: "check my order", "order status", "did my payment go through", "status of sessio

labels.name: subscribe_newsletter
labels.description:
Subscribe an email address to the Eveoy newsletter (case studies + lookbooks).

Use this when the user EXPLICITLY asks to subscribe or sign up for updates.

Trigger phrases include: "subscribe me", "sign me up for the newsletter", "add my email to updates", "join the Eveoy list".

Returns: { ok: tru

labels.name: start_checkout
labels.description: Create an Eveoy checkout and return a payment link. Pricing mirrors the order page: $24.99 per verified customer base, plus two options — a guaranteed purchase (guarantee_type "visit_purchase": every shopper buys your chosen SKU at your register; you add the SKU price in cents, tax included, $5–$100

labels.name: capture_profile
labels.description:
Save the company an agent represents so Eveoy can tailor recommendations and have the team follow up. Records first-party business-contact details (no consumer data) as a lead.

Use this when the user wants to:
- Tell Eveoy which brand or company they represent
- Get tailored pilot recommendations o

labels.name: book_demo
labels.description:
Get the link to book a live Eveoy demo, and flag the request to the Eveoy team.

Use this when the user wants to:
- Schedule a demo or walkthrough
- Talk to the Eveoy team

Trigger phrases include: "book a demo", "schedule a call", "talk to sales", "get a walkthrough".

Pass contact_name, work_email

labels.name: request_human
labels.description:
Hand off to a human on the Eveoy team and flag the conversation for follow-up.

Use this when the user wants to:
- Talk to a person or a sales rep (not just book a demo slot)
- Get help the other tools cannot give
- Escalate a question or ask for a callback

Trigger phrases: "talk to a human", "conn

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "subscribe_newsletter" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Subscribe an email address to the Eveoy newsletter (case studies + lookbooks). Use this when the user EXPLICITLY asks to subscribe or sign up for updates. Trigg…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_pricing, get_app_link, get_case_studies, subscribe_newsletter, book_demo · Private data: capture_profile, book_demo · Public sink: subscribe_newsletter.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_pricing, get_app_link, get_case_studies, subscribe_newsletter, book_demo · Private data: capture_profile, book_demo · Public sink: subscribe_newsletter

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

detail: Tool "book_demo" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet:
before: "(hash bce0c8d3)" → after: "Get the link to book a live Eveoy demo, and flag the request to the Eveoy team.

Use this when the user wants to:
- Schedule a demo or walkthrough
- Talk to the Eveoy team

Trigger phrases include: "book a demo", "schedule a call", "talk to sales", "get a walkthrough".

Pass contact_name, work_email"

remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
